---
name: Caveat (disclosed limitation)
about: A disclosed, accepted limitation — a record, filed CLOSED (never an open loop)
title: ''
labels: caveat
---

<!-- A caveat is NOT an open concern to revisit — that framing pretends the decision world
     is closed when in science it isn't. A caveat here is a *record* of an accepted,
     disclosed limitation, with no to-do, so CLOSE it on creation (reason: completed),
     exactly like a result. Open = an open loop that needs attention; a disclosed
     limitation needs none.

     Before filing, ask which of these it really is:
       - a RISK to a specific objective ("2 ns may be too short for #14")  -> don't file a
         caveat; fold it into that objective's "What would make this wrong" section.
       - an improvement you intend to pursue                               -> it's an
         OBJECTIVE; file it as one.
       - a genuinely accepted limitation the README must disclose          -> file THIS
         record and close it.
     See CLAUDE.md. -->

<!-- One-line statement of the limitation. -->

<!-- Which results, hits, conclusions are affected; how to flag outputs that depend on this. -->

## Interpretation

<!-- How to read framework outputs given this limitation — concrete consumer-facing guidance
     ("treat ranking as ordinal, not magnitude", "don't trust the sign", "quote with the
     s777 caveat", etc.). -->

## Plain English

<!-- 2-4 sentences for a reader outside the project. What the limitation is and what it
     means for trusting the numbers. No jargon, no acronyms. -->
